content: We can reframe Self-Refine’s single-stream iterative self-improvement framework as a base that can be augmented with multi-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms to unlock stronger refinement without adding external data or training. Specifically, instead of having the LLM only critique its own single initial output each iteration, we can have it generate multiple divergent solutions first, then use Self-Contrast style inconsistent perspective comparison to generate higher-quality self-feedback for each iteration of refinement.
